relatedness_reasoning: The inbound file contains pre-approved credit offers with customer and loan terms; the outbound file processes monthly repayments from approved loans. Both share customer_id, account_number, currency, and loan financial concepts. The Credit system receives offers and later sends repayment instructions to Accounts, forming a plausible pipeline where approved offers become active loans with scheduled repayments.

mappings.reasoning: No equivalent identifier exists in inbound; this is a system-generated batch identifier for repayment processing.
Batch processing date is generated at repayment execution time, not derived from the inbound offer.
Inbound department_to='credit' becomes outbound department.from='Credit', indicating Credit is now the originating department.
Accounts department is the next system in the pipeline; not specified in the inbound offer data.
Direct copy of customer's bank account number for direct debit collection.
Same customer identifier, renamed from customer_id to customerId in array context.
Full customer name constructed by combining first_name and last_name fields.
Inbound offer_id becomes loanId in outbound after the offer is approved and converted to an active loan.
Direct copy of the fixed monthly payment amount from the credit offer terms.
Currency code copied directly from the credit offer (GBP).
Due date is calculated based on loan activation and repayment schedule, not present in the inbound offer data.
Direct debit mandate is established during loan approval process; not present in the inbound pre-approval offer.
Repayment status is determined at execution time; inbound only contains offer status pending_credit_review.
Repayment type 'monthly' is inferred from loan structure but not explicitly stated in inbound offer data.
Sum of all monthly_payment amounts across the repayment batch; aggregated from multiple offer records.
Count of repayment records in the batch is a metadata field generated during batch assembly.
Batch generation timestamp is created at repayment processing time, not derived from inbound offer timestamp.
